author: nflaig
content: if we queue builder deposits it would also allow us to remove `is_pending_validator` check added in https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4897, which is a bit tricky to implement in a performant way

author: nflaig
content: <@425572898787426305> I think with current spec we will need to validate each signature to know whether it's for pending validator or not, I don't see a way around that

author: m.kalinin
content: we will still have to do this check, right?

author: m.kalinin
content: but it can be done upon dequeue pending builders

author: nflaig
content: if we move them to the queue it's same as for validators today

author: nflaig
content: ie. rate limited by the queue

author: m.kalinin
content: oh, I see. I mean a separate queue, separate queue would make sense because if it would be a single queue then builders will be affected by the activation queue limit

author: nflaig
content: ah yes, would need separate queue

author: nflaig
content: I think the only reason as <@808969530608451584> pointed out already why we don't have this is to have builders at the fork boundary

author: m.kalinin
content: btw, if we introduce another queue then we would need to run `is_pending_builder` for new validator processing

author: m.kalinin
content: one potential attack vector is to delay builder activation by front running it with a validator with the same pubkey at a cost of 1 ETH -- i think it's too costly to be a real attack vector

author: potuz
content: This is being discussed by different people in different channels. At this stage I feel the sanest option is to just limit the number of deposits to whatever we can handle. I do not hold this strongly though just we have to acknowledge that any change like this will delay the fork. At any rate this has to be discussed in one place and not so many channels and even threads.

author: nflaig
content: I am not really convinced that the current spec should be changed for this, we need to limit how many deposits EL pack one way or the other to avoid the deadlock brought up here https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1500774070621306910/1501242008508043306, if we wanna increase the gas limit further. But I would rather have them use a more sane limit like 1024, 512 (or even lower). If a builder puts in 8192 deposits in their payload they can see it being orphaned, I don't see an issue with that.

author: barnabasbusa
content: we are good to limit to 512 imho

author: barnabasbusa
content: there has been only 2 slots with more than that

author: jtraglia
content: <@425572898787426305> are you looking into spec changes for this? I'd be interested to see what you come up with.

author: jtraglia
content: But right now, I do lean towards keeping the spec as-is. Introducing another queue would be pretty complex.

author: m.kalinin
content: I would remove unnecessary sig verification for `is_pending_validator` but that's orthogonal to a separate builder's queue. If we decide to limit to 512 then we have to reduce `MAX_DEPOSIT_REQUESTS_PER_PAYLOAD` to `512` and then each EL client will have to check this number

author: jtraglia
content: But it's not unnecessary. It is there for a reason.

author: etan_status
content: as discussed in TG, for regular deposits, that doesn't equate to 512 signatures pre-gloas.

author: etan_status
content: the resource that should be rate-limited is the total number of signature checks in the beacon block

author: etan_status
content: especially deposits, because they require proof-of-possession check when creating a new validator, so they cannot be fast-aggregated

author: etan_status
content: pre-gloas, the processing on the CL is just a memcpy: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/electra/beacon-chain.md#new-process_deposit_request -- it doesn't need a rate-limit

author: etan_status
content: the proper solution would be a rate-limit for builder registrations

author: etan_status
content: like, we had this entire Electra attestation work where we deliberately reduced the number of attestations to make the blocks faster to zk verify (for some hypothetical L2 / research work etc), and now we are willing to add a multiple of that back into the protocol

author: etan_status
content: also, for the UX side, it would probably be cleaner if builder-deposits would only "activate" on finality anyway. otherwise, the pubkey -> builder-index becomes fork-specific

author: etan_status
content: like, you refresh the page on beaconcha.in, and then suddenly your link points to a different key

author: jtraglia
content: Part of this is how it works now. A builder is not activated until its deposit is finalized.

author: jtraglia
content: This is an interesting idea. This could work.

author: etan_status
content: ok, then that's already good. pubkey -> index assignment only on finality is great if that's how it works.

author: jtraglia
content: Well technically it can be assigned an index before it's finalized. But we can guarantee bids from active builders will have static indices.

author: etan_status
content:
yeah, maybe a simple queue alike in spirit to the existing ones, rather than a novel concept.
that allows us to reason about the 8192 serialization limit (just drop it) independently of the max BLS signature count per BeaconBlock (low dozens max)

author: jtraglia
content: I'm not sure if we should introduce another queue unless it's absolutely necessary.

author: jtraglia
content: But yeah, maybe. I'd like to see what it looks like before stating a real opinion.

author: etan_status
content: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7549 --> this was the E* work that reduced the BLS count

author: etan_status
content: yeah, just tossing in ideas
